scenes.narration: This is the Floor Tech app — built for technicians working on the shop floor. It runs on bay tablets and phones. Big touch targets, glove-friendly, and offline-first.
Sign-in mirrors the ERP — email first, Google single-sign-on for workspace accounts. For the walkthrough we'll use mock mode with a technician role.
Four tabs along the bottom. Queue, Shift, Time, and Sync. Everything a tech does during a shift lives under one of these four.
You start the shift on the Shift tab. Tap Clock In to mark this tablet as yours. Then set your availability — Available, On break, or Needs help. Dispatch sees the change immediately.
The Queue tab is your work list. Each card is a work order with a number, a material-readiness badge, a sync-status badge, and the next action you need to take.
Tapping a card opens the work order detail. The checklist is the heart of the page — tap a checkbox to mark a task done. Everything updates immediately, even offline.
Add a note for the next tech, upload a photo as evidence, or request additional parts if you realize something is missing. Every change gets a small sync badge — pending, synced, or needs retry.

If something stops you — missing parts, a broken tool, a customer hold — tap Mark Blocked, pick a reason code, and add a sentence of detail. The work order moves off your queue and onto the shop manager's blocked list.
The Time tab has one big button — Start Timer. Tap it when you pick up a work order, tap it again when you put it down. Manual entries cover the times you forget.
When Wi-Fi drops, the banner at the top turns yellow. Every tap still works — updates stack up locally. When you're back online, the app replays them automatically. Nothing is ever lost.
The Sync tab is where you triage queued updates. Queued, needs retry, and failed counts across the top. Replay Now forces a retry when auto-replay hasn't caught up.
Bay tablets are shared — clock out when you finish your shift so the next tech starts fresh. Everything you did is logged against your name.
That's the Floor Tech app. For the manager's view of the same work orders, or for anything beyond execution, see the ERP walkthrough.
